title: Understand multiplication using groups of objects
description: Practice representing multiplication as equal groups, repeated addition, or arrays.
topic_path: Understand multiplication using groups of objects

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which image shows $3\times2$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blueD3 \times \maroonD2$ equals $\blueD3$ groups of $\maroonD2$.

Which image shows $\blueD3$ groups of $\maroonD2$?
This image shows $\blueD3$ groups with $\maroonD2$ horses in each group.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

The following image shows $\blueD3\times\maroonD2$:

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression could we use to calculate the number of blossoms?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 4]]

[[☃ image 5]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can write multiplication expressions as the following:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}\times\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$
There are $\blueD5$ groups of flowers.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 4]]

[[☃ image 5]]

There are $\maroonD7$ flowers in each group.  

[[☃ image 1]]

So, there are $\blueD5$ groups of $\maroonD{{7}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$+$ 
[[☃ image 2]]
$+$ 
[[☃ image 3]]
$+$ 
[[☃ image 4]]
$+$ 
[[☃ image 5]]

$\blueD5$ groups of $\maroonD7$ is $\blueD5 \times \maroonD7$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression could we use to calculate the number of ladybugs?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can write multiplication expressions as the following:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}\times\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$
There are $\blueD3$ groups of ladybugs.

[[☃ image 1]]

There are $\maroonD{9}$ ladybugs in each group.  

[[☃ image 1]]

So, there are $\blueD3$ groups of $\maroonD{{9}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD3$ groups of $\maroonD{9}$ is $\blueD3 \times \maroonD{9}$.

options.alt: Three groups of 9 ladybugs. Each group of ladybugs has a circle around it.
